records.ety: [OE. bosch, busch, buysch, bosk, busk; akin to D. bosch, OHG. busc, G. busch, Icel. būskr, būski, Dan. busk, Sw. buske, and also to LL. boscus, buscus, Pr. bosc, It. bosco, Sp. & Pg. bosque, F. bois, OF. bos. Whether the LL. or G. form is the original is uncertain; if the LL., it is perh. from the same source as E. box a case. Cf. Ambush, Boscage, Bouquet, Box a case.]

defs: A thicket, or place abounding in trees or shrubs; a wild forest.
notes: ☞ This was the original sense of the word, as in the Dutch bosch, a wood, and was so used by Chaucer. In this sense it is extensively used in the British colonies, especially at the Cape of Good Hope, and also in Australia and Canada; as, to live or settle in the bush.

defs: A shrub; esp., a shrub with branches rising from or near the root; a thick shrub or a cluster of shrubs.
quotes: To bind a bush of thorns among sweet-smelling flowers.

defs: A shrub cut off, or a shrublike branch of a tree; as, bushes to support pea vines.
A shrub or branch, properly, a branch of ivy (as sacred to Bacchus), hung out at vintners' doors, or as a tavern sign; hence, a tavern sign, and symbolically, the tavern itself.
quotes: If it be true that good wine needs no bush, 't is true that a good play needs no epilogue.

defs: The tail, or brush, of a fox.
To branch thickly in the manner of a bush.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bushed (bsht); p. pr. & vb. n. Bushing.]

defs: To set bushes for; to support with bushes; as, to bush peas.
To use a bush harrow on (land), for covering seeds sown; to harrow with a bush; as, to bush a piece of land; to bush seeds into the ground.

records.ety: [D. bus a box, akin to E. box; or F. boucher to plug.]

defs: A lining for a hole to make it smaller; a thimble or ring of metal or wood inserted in a plate or other part of machinery to receive the wear of a pivot or arbor.
notes: ☞ In the larger machines, such a piece is called a box, particularly in the United States.

defs: A piece of copper, screwed into a gun, through which the venthole is bored.
To furnish with a bush, or lining; as, to bush a pivot hole.
